summary: fallbackSanitizer regex in markdown.ts line 43 corrupted parenthesized permission keys like verify(action="files")
details: FIXED: Modified regex from `/^([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*)\s*:\s*(.*)$/` to `/^([a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*(?:\([^)]*\))?)\s*:\s*(.*)$/` to capture optional parenthesized action suffix. Root cause: duplicate key in secretary agent YAML triggered fallback sanitizer; original regex split keys like `verify(action="files")` into key=verify, value=(action="files"): "allow", corrupting the permission.

summary: Permission evaluate() and disabled() in packages/core/src/permission.ts prefer last-match over specificity, allowing catch-all to override specific deny
details: FIXED: Both evaluate() and disabled() used `findLast` which gives priority to whatever rule appears last in the merged ruleset, regardless of specificity. A catch-all `*: "allow"` rule from session config would override a specific `tune: "deny"` rule from agent config. Fixed by preferring exact permission-name match over wildcard match in both functions. This resolves the "tune: deny not resolving" bug.

summary: Orphan permission keys (like tune) silently ignored when they don't match any registered tool ID — no warning is emitted
details: No warning is emitted when a permission key in the agent config doesn't match any registered tool. Agent authors can define permissions that are silently dead config. Consider adding validation in fromConfig() or a warning log for orphaned permission keys. This affects the GM agent's `tune: "deny"` which resolves correctly now (if tune is a registered tool or MCP tool at runtime) but would silently be ignored if no tool named tune is registered.

summary: Agent config normalize() in agent.ts uses Object.assign for permission merge, losing type safety for custom permission keys
details: The normalize() function declares `permission: ConfigPermission.Info = {}` then `Object.assign(permission, agent.permission)`. The ConfigPermission.Info type may not include an index signature for custom keys like coordinate(action="send"), making the type system blind to these keys. Consider widening the type or using a type-safe spread.
